# cavimg README Showcase GIF Implementation Plan

> **For agentic workers:** REQUIRED SUB-SKILL: Use superpowers:subagent-driven-development (recommended) or superpowers:executing-plans to implement this plan task-by-task. Steps use checkbox (`- [ ]`) syntax for tracking.

**Goal:** Add an animated GIF to the top of `README.md` demonstrating cavimg's protection in motion, generated by a reproducible script.

**Architecture:** A scripted in-page demo (`examples/vite/showcase.html` + `showcase.ts`) renders a real `<img>` and a real `<cav-img>` side by side over a stylized SVG, with a deterministic `window.__frame(n)` that poses cursor/menu/chips per frame. A Node script (`scripts/make-showcase-gif.mjs`) drives it with Playwright, screenshots each frame, and encodes `images/showcase.gif` via `gifenc` (+`pngjs` to decode frames). README embeds the GIF.

**Tech Stack:** Vite (reuse the example app's dev server to resolve `import 'cavimg'`), Playwright (chromium, already installed in `e2e`), `gifenc` + `pngjs` (pure-JS dev-deps).

**Design:** `docs/superpowers/specs/2026-07-23-cavimg-readme-showcase-design.md`. This continues the `feature/examples-e2e-and-docs` branch (Plan 2, already reviewed merge-ready).

## Global Constraints

- The demo page is a SECOND Vite entry in `examples/vite` (`showcase.html` at the app root); it does NOT affect the E2E specs (which target `/`).
- `window.__frame(n)` is a PURE function of `n` (cursor xy, menu/chip/tag visibility) — no wall-clock timers — so capture is reproducible. `window.__ready === true` once cavimg has loaded the right panel.
- GIF: ~**880×360**, **24 frames**, ~**3s** loop (delay ≈ 125 ms/frame, loop forever), committed at `images/showcase.gif`, target ≤ ~500 KB.
- `gifenc` + `pngjs` are **dev-only**, added to the `@cavimg/e2e` package — never to `packages/cavimg`.
- No changes to `packages/cavimg`, the E2E specs, `gen-fixture.mjs`, or the E2E fixture.
- Build the library before generating the GIF (`pnpm --filter cavimg build`).
- README states the demo overlay (cursor/menu/chips) illustrates the real, E2E-verified behavior.
- Commits carry NO `Co-Authored-By` trailer.

## File Structure

```
cavimg/
├─ examples/vite/
│  ├─ public/showcase-art.svg      (Task 1) the artwork
│  ├─ showcase.html                (Task 1) second Vite entry
│  └─ src/showcase.ts              (Task 1) demo stage + window.__frame
├─ scripts/make-showcase-gif.mjs   (Task 2) Playwright → gifenc
├─ e2e/package.json                (Task 2, modify) add gifenc + pngjs + `showcase` script
├─ images/showcase.gif             (Task 2) committed output
├─ examples/angular/src/app/app.spec.ts (Task 3, modify) fix stale assertion
└─ README.md                       (Task 3, modify) add GIF hero
```

### Task 1: Demo stage (SVG art + showcase page)

**Files:**
- Create: `examples/vite/public/showcase-art.svg`, `examples/vite/showcase.html`, `examples/vite/src/showcase.ts`

**Interfaces:**
- Produces: a page at `/showcase.html` exposing `window.__ready: boolean` and `window.__frame(n: number): void` (0–23), with a stage clipped to 880×360 at the top-left of the viewport.


- [ ] **Step 2: Create `examples/vite/showcase.html`**

```html
<!doctype html>
<html lang="en">
  <head>
    <meta charset="UTF-8" />
    <meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1.0" />
    <title>cavimg — showcase</title>
    <style>
      html,body{margin:0;background:#0b1020;font-family:ui-sans-serif,system-ui,Segoe UI,Roboto,sans-serif}
      #stage{position:relative;width:880px;height:360px;overflow:hidden;background:#0b1020;color:#e7ecf5}
      .panels{display:flex;gap:24px;padding:20px}
      .panel{flex:1;display:flex;flex-direction:column;gap:8px}
      .panel h2{margin:0;font-size:14px;font-weight:600;letter-spacing:.3px}
      .art{position:relative;width:100%;height:236px;border-radius:12px;overflow:hidden;box-shadow:0 6px 24px rgba(0,0,0,.4)}
      .art img,.art cav-img{display:block;width:100%;height:236px}
      .tag{font-size:12px;line-height:1.35;min-height:34px;padding:6px 10px;border-radius:8px}
      .tag.bad{background:#3a1220;color:#ffb4c0;border:1px solid #7a2233}
      .tag.good{background:#0f2e1e;color:#9be8bf;border:1px solid #1f6b45}
      .tag.neutral{background:#141b30;color:#9aa6bf;border:1px solid #263149}
      #cursor{position:absolute;width:22px;height:22px;pointer-events:none;transition:none;filter:drop-shadow(0 1px 2px rgba(0,0,0,.6));z-index:5}
      #menu{position:absolute;z-index:6;background:#f7f8fb;color:#1b2130;border-radius:8px;box-shadow:0 10px 30px rgba(0,0,0,.5);font-size:12px;overflow:hidden;display:none}
      #menu div{padding:7px 14px}
      #menu div.hi{background:#dfe7fb}
      #menu.blocked{text-decoration:line-through;opacity:.55}
      #chip{position:absolute;z-index:6;display:none;background:#111;color:#fff;border:1px solid #333;border-radius:999px;padding:6px 12px;font-size:13px;font-weight:600}
    </style>
  </head>
  <body>
    <div id="stage">
      <div class="panels">
        <div class="panel">
          <h2>Plain &lt;img&gt;</h2>
          <div class="art"><img id="plain" src="/showcase-art.svg" alt="art (plain img)" /></div>
          <div class="tag neutral" id="tag-left">a normal image</div>
        </div>
        <div class="panel">
          <h2>&lt;cav-img&gt;</h2>
          <div class="art"><cav-img id="prot" src="/showcase-art.svg" alt="art (protected)"></cav-img></div>
          <div class="tag neutral" id="tag-right">rendered to canvas</div>
        </div>
      </div>
      <svg id="cursor" viewBox="0 0 24 24" fill="#fff" stroke="#111" stroke-width="1.2"><path d="M4 2 L4 20 L9 15 L12 22 L15 21 L12 14 L19 14 Z"/></svg>
      <div id="menu"><div class="hi">Save image as…</div><div>Copy image</div><div>Open image in new tab</div></div>
      <div id="chip">🚫 blocked</div>
    </div>
    <script type="module" src="/src/showcase.ts"></script>
  </body>
</html>
```

- [ ] **Step 3: Create `examples/vite/src/showcase.ts`**

```ts
import 'cavimg';

declare global {
  interface Window {
    __ready?: boolean;
    __frame?: (n: number) => void;
  }
}

const FRAMES = 24;
const cursor = document.getElementById('cursor')!;
const menu = document.getElementById('menu')!;
const chip = document.getElementById('chip')!;
const tagL = document.getElementById('tag-left')!;
const tagR = document.getElementById('tag-right')!;

// Anchor points within the 880x360 stage.
const START = { x: 430, y: 330 };
const LEFT = { x: 210, y: 150 };   // over the plain image
const RIGHT = { x: 660, y: 150 };  // over the cav-img

const lerp = (a: number, b: number, t: number) => a + (b - a) * Math.max(0, Math.min(1, t));
function place(el: HTMLElement, x: number, y: number) { el.style.left = `${x}px`; el.style.top = `${y}px`; }

function reset() {
  menu.style.display = 'none';
  menu.classList.remove('blocked');
  chip.style.display = 'none';
  tagL.className = 'tag neutral'; tagL.textContent = 'a normal image';
  tagR.className = 'tag neutral'; tagR.textContent = 'rendered to canvas';
}

// Pure function of n: pose the whole scene. No timers.
window.__frame = (n: number) => {
  reset();
  if (n < 6) {
    // glide from start toward the left image
    place(cursor, lerp(START.x, LEFT.x, n / 6), lerp(START.y, LEFT.y, n / 6));
  } else if (n < 12) {
    // right-click the plain image → menu opens, bad tag
    place(cursor, LEFT.x, LEFT.y);
    menu.style.display = 'block';
    place(menu, LEFT.x + 6, LEFT.y + 6);
    tagL.className = 'tag bad';
    tagL.textContent = '❌ saveable · draggable · URL in inspector';
  } else if (n < 18) {
    // glide to the cav-img
    const t = (n - 12) / 6;
    place(cursor, lerp(LEFT.x, RIGHT.x, t), lerp(LEFT.y, RIGHT.y, t));
  } else {
    // right-click the cav-img → blocked
    place(cursor, RIGHT.x, RIGHT.y);
    chip.style.display = 'block';
    place(chip, RIGHT.x - 30, RIGHT.y - 10);
    tagR.className = 'tag good';
    tagR.textContent = '✅ no <img> · no src · save & drag blocked';
  }
};

const prot = document.getElementById('prot')!;
const finish = () => { window.__frame!(0); window.__ready = true; };
prot.addEventListener('cav-load', finish, { once: true });
prot.addEventListener('cav-error', finish, { once: true }); // still pose the scene if load fails
// fallback so __ready is never stuck
setTimeout(() => { if (!window.__ready) finish(); }, 4000);
```

```bash
pnpm --filter cavimg build
# start vite in the background, then probe
pnpm --filter @cavimg/example-vite dev &
sleep 4
curl -sSf http://localhost:5173/showcase.html >/dev/null && echo "showcase.html served"
# stop the background vite (kill the dev server process/port 5173) before continuing
```
Expected: `showcase.html served`. (A deeper visual check happens in Task 2 when the GIF is produced.)

- [ ] **Step 5: Commit**

```bash
git add examples/vite/public/showcase-art.svg examples/vite/showcase.html examples/vite/src/showcase.ts
git commit -m "feat: add showcase demo stage page for vite example"
```

### Task 2: GIF generator + produce `images/showcase.gif`

**Files:**
- Modify: `e2e/package.json` (add `gifenc`, `pngjs` devDeps + `showcase` script)
- Create: `scripts/make-showcase-gif.mjs`
- Output: `images/showcase.gif`

**Interfaces:**
- Consumes: `/showcase.html` (`window.__ready`, `window.__frame`) from Task 1.
- Produces: `images/showcase.gif`.

- [ ] **Step 1: Add deps + script** — from repo root:

```bash
pnpm --filter @cavimg/e2e add -D gifenc pngjs
```
Then add to `e2e/package.json` `"scripts"`: `"showcase": "node ../scripts/make-showcase-gif.mjs"`.

- [ ] **Step 2: Create `scripts/make-showcase-gif.mjs`**

```js
import { spawn } from 'node:child_process';
import { writeFileSync } from 'node:fs';
import { fileURLToPath } from 'node:url';
import { chromium } from '@playwright/test';
import { GIFEncoder, quantize, applyPalette } from 'gifenc';
import { PNG } from 'pngjs';

const URL = 'http://localhost:5173/showcase.html';
const OUT = fileURLToPath(new URL('../images/showcase.gif', import.meta.url));
const FRAMES = 24;
const CLIP = { x: 0, y: 0, width: 880, height: 360 };

function waitForServer(url, timeoutMs = 60000) {
  const start = Date.now();
  return new Promise((resolve, reject) => {
    const tick = async () => {
      try {
        const r = await fetch(url);
        if (r.ok) return resolve();
      } catch { /* not up yet */ }
      if (Date.now() - start > timeoutMs) return reject(new Error('vite dev server did not start'));
      setTimeout(tick, 500);
    };
    tick();
  });
}

const server = spawn('pnpm', ['--filter', '@cavimg/example-vite', 'dev'], {
  stdio: 'ignore', shell: process.platform === 'win32',
});
try {
  await waitForServer(URL);
  const browser = await chromium.launch();
  const page = await browser.newPage({ viewport: { width: 900, height: 380 }, deviceScaleFactor: 1 });
  await page.goto(URL, { waitUntil: 'load' });
  await page.waitForFunction(() => window.__ready === true, { timeout: 30000 });

  const gif = GIFEncoder();
  for (let n = 0; n < FRAMES; n++) {
    await page.evaluate((i) => window.__frame(i), n);
    await page.evaluate(() => new Promise((r) => requestAnimationFrame(() => requestAnimationFrame(r))));
    const buf = await page.screenshot({ clip: CLIP });
    const png = PNG.sync.read(buf);
    const rgba = new Uint8Array(png.data.buffer, png.data.byteOffset, png.data.length);
    const palette = quantize(rgba, 256);
    const index = applyPalette(rgba, palette);
    gif.writeFrame(index, png.width, png.height, { palette, delay: Math.round(3000 / FRAMES), repeat: n === 0 ? 0 : undefined });
  }
  gif.finish();
  writeFileSync(OUT, Buffer.from(gif.bytes()));
  console.log(`wrote ${OUT} (${gif.bytes().length} bytes, ${FRAMES} frames)`);
  await browser.close();
} finally {
  server.kill();
}
```

- [ ] **Step 3: Build the library and generate the GIF**

```bash
pnpm --filter cavimg build
pnpm --filter @cavimg/e2e run showcase
```
Expected: `wrote …/images/showcase.gif (<N> bytes, 24 frames)`, file size under ~500 KB. If a stray process holds port 5173, kill it first.

- [ ] **Step 4: Verify the GIF** — confirm it exists, is a valid non-trivial GIF, and (controller step) view it:

```bash
ls -la images/showcase.gif
node -e "const b=require('fs').readFileSync('images/showcase.gif'); console.log('magic', b.slice(0,6).toString('latin1'), 'bytes', b.length)"
```
Expected: magic `GIF89a`, bytes < ~512000. The controller reads `images/showcase.gif` to confirm it shows the two-panel demo (art + cursor/menu/chip), not a blank frame. If blank or the layout is wrong, fix the demo/clip before proceeding.

- [ ] **Step 5: Commit**

```bash
git add e2e/package.json pnpm-lock.yaml scripts/make-showcase-gif.mjs images/showcase.gif
git commit -m "feat: add reproducible showcase gif generator and output"
```

## Self-Review

**Spec coverage:** §3 storyboard → Task 1 `__frame` beats; §4 SVG art → Task 1 Step 1; §5 components (demo page + gif script) → Tasks 1–2; §6 deps (gifenc+pngjs, dev-only in e2e) → Task 2 Step 1; §7 README hero + reframe → Task 3; §8 Angular test fix → Task 3 Step 3; §10 honesty caption → Task 3 Step 1. ✅

**Placeholder scan:** every code step has full content; commands have expected output. The `<N>`/`~500 KB` are runtime-measured outputs, not placeholders.

**Type/interface consistency:** `window.__ready` / `window.__frame(n)` declared in `showcase.ts` and consumed by `make-showcase-gif.mjs` (`window.__ready === true`, `window.__frame(i)`). Stage clip 880×360 matches the `#stage` CSS size and the GIF `CLIP`. `@cavimg/example-vite` filter name matches Plan 2's vite package. Frame count 24 consistent across page anchors, script loop, and delay math.

**Risk note (for execution):** the `gifenc`/`pngjs` API calls (`quantize`/`applyPalette`/`writeFrame`, `PNG.sync.read`) are the most likely spot needing a small correction at runtime — the implementer must actually run Step 3 and confirm a valid `GIF89a` output, and the controller visually confirms the frame content.
